parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now answer the questions on your screen.

Speaker 2: Great! I feel much better now. Oh, you're also gonna have to help me with the bibliography. I think I did it wrong last term. My professor wasn't very happy.

Speaker 1: Yeah, they all say they're very important. It's easy, really. Just takes a while. It helps if you do it as you go along. So when we do the research, make a note of all the information that you need.

Speaker 2: That's a good idea. So we need the author's name and the title of the chapter.

Speaker 1: Oh no, you don't need the chapter title, just the title of the book.

Speaker 2: Oh, really? And what about page numbers?

Speaker 1: No, not all the time. They are only important if we give direct quotes, and we won't be doing that.

Speaker 2: Okay, is that all?

Speaker 1: No, you also need to give the year of publication.

Speaker 2: Where do you find that?

Speaker 1: On the first page, usually next to the ISBN number. Oh, and you need the publisher's name.

Speaker 2: Got it. Why is this so important for this presentation anyway?

Speaker 1: It's because of the assessment. We get a mark for our research, so we need to show evidence of what we've done.

Speaker 2: Really? What else do we get marked on?

Speaker 1: Let's see. In my last presentation, we received a mark for delivery, pace, and organisation.

Speaker 2: Wow, that's a lot to think about.

Speaker 1: Yeah, but it's more simple this time. The only other area we'll be marked on is content.

Speaker 2: OK, so we only get two marks?

Speaker 1: Yeah, that's right. Anyway, should we get going?
